summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of 'Paired data'?

"Paired data" means: Two measurements on the same subject or matched subjects, creating natural pairs whose differences are analyzed. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Paired t-test'?

"Paired t-test" means: One-sample t-test applied to the differences: t = d̄/(sd/√n) with df = n-1, testing H₀: μd = 0. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Mean difference (d̄)'?

"Mean difference (d̄)" means: The average of the paired differences; estimates the true mean difference μd between the two conditions. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Paired t-interval'?

"Paired t-interval" means: d̄ ± t*·(sd/√n); estimates the true mean difference between two paired conditions. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Advantage of pairing'?

"Advantage of pairing" means: Reduces variability by eliminating between-subject variation, making it easier to detect true treatment effects. The other options describe different concepts.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: Which of the following best defines "Advantage of pairing"?

"Advantage of pairing" is defined as: Reduces variability by eliminating between-subject variation, making it easier to detect true treatment effects. The other options describe different concepts.

Example: Which term is described by: "The average of the paired differences; estimates the true mean difference μd between the two conditions"?

This description refers to "Mean difference (d̄)".

"Advantage of pairing" means: Reduces variability by eliminating between-subject variation, making it easier to detect true treatment effects. The other options describe different concepts.
